disclaimer: Demonstration content only — not for real-world navigation.

sections.title: Fern Gap to Paper Ridge
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional rolling ridge walk used to demonstrate northbound section notes.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional gradual descent used to demonstrate southbound section notes.
sections.elevationNotes: Demo profile: two short climbs and one broad saddle.
sections.waterNotes: No real water information. Carry what your actual plan requires.
sections.campingNotes: Fictional campsite references are not usable locations.
sections.seasonalNotes: Demo note: leaf cover changes visibility.
sections.navigationCautions: This section and every coordinate are fabricated.
sections.historicalNotes: Invented fieldbook narrative with no historical claim.
sections.fieldNotes: A quiet place in the prototype to learn the guide layout.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1

sections.title: Paper Ridge to Stone Wren Hollow
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional descent through mixed hardwoods.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional steady climb toward an open ridge.
sections.waterNotes: The named spring is fictional and must not be sought.
sections.seasonalNotes: Demo note: muddy after imagined rain.
sections.historicalNotes: The Stone Wren name is invented.
sections.fieldNotes: Use category filters to find the fictional spring entry.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1

sections.title: Stone Wren Hollow to Laurel Lantern
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional longer section with alternating coves and knobs.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional technical descent followed by a broad valley walk.
sections.campingNotes: No legal camping guidance is provided.
sections.navigationCautions: The caution markers teach UI behavior only.
sections.historicalNotes: No real people or events are represented.
sections.fieldNotes: The longest fictional section in the seed bundle.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1

sections.title: Laurel Lantern to Quiet Anvil
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional traverse beneath a rounded summit.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional shaded walk toward a laurel cove.
sections.campingNotes: The shelter-like marker is a fictional icon.
sections.historicalNotes: Quiet Anvil is an invented place name.
sections.fieldNotes: Designed to demonstrate confidence and provenance badges.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1

sections.title: Quiet Anvil to Hemlock Window
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional creek-side approach and ridge finish.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional ridge departure and creek-side finish.
sections.elevationNotes: Demo profile: uneven grades and a steep finish.
sections.seasonalNotes: Demo note: imagined creek crossings vary.
sections.navigationCautions: Coordinates intentionally do not represent trail features.
sections.fieldNotes: Use this section to test strenuous filtering.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1

sections.title: Hemlock Window to Copper Moon
sections.northboundSummary: A fictional compact closing section across low ridges.
sections.southboundSummary: A fictional welcoming first section with a short climb.
sections.waterNotes: No water source exists at the demo marker.
sections.fieldNotes: A short section for completion-flow demonstrations.
sections.sourceCitation: MacKaye Fieldbook fictional seed set v1
